purpose: Compose the clean main-based blocklist consensus hardening workstream with the frozen Redis dashboard stack for full-stack runtime testing, without merging redis-dash-0-0-2 history into the main-bound hardening branch.

gitops_policy.desired_state_rule: Deployable test state is defined by this manifest and exact repository SHAs, not by unpinned moving branch heads.

blockdag-corechain.role: active blocklist consensus hardening implementation source
blockdag-corechain.update_rule: Advance test_commit as hardening PR slices land on blocklist-consensus-hardening. Final PR target remains main.

pool.role: frozen current pool runtime support for Redis dashboard full-stack testing
pool.update_rule: Do not advance for blocklist work unless a pool-specific compatibility change is required and reviewed separately.

redis-dash.role: frozen Redis dashboard/control-plane runtime for full-stack testing
redis-dash.update_rule: Do not advance for consensus hardening unless dashboard visibility changes are explicitly required.

stack.role: GitOps desired-state owner and integration-test composition repo
stack.test_commit: the git commit containing this manifest
stack.update_rule: Update this manifest through PR-reviewed commits when repository SHAs change for integration testing.

implementation: Create small task branches from blocklist-consensus-hardening, or commit directly to blocklist-consensus-hardening only when the work is narrow and controlled.
Run targeted tests for each slice.
Update this manifest only when a new tested full-stack composition is intended.
testing: Checkout each repo to the test_ref/test_commit listed here.
Build from source using stack scripts.
Run the blocklist hardening test matrix from /home/jeremy/Documents/blockdag-corechain-blocklist-hardening-20260627/07-test-matrix-and-acceptance.html.
Record test evidence before updating release tags or deployment manifests.
release: Open final PRs from blocklist-consensus-hardening to main after the hardening work passes consensus, txpool, RPC/indexer, and full-stack tests.
Create immutable tags/releases only from tested commits.
Do not reuse redis-dash-0-0-2 for future consensus work.

acceptance.clean_pr_to_main: Blocklist hardening PRs to main must not include unrelated Redis dashboard history.
acceptance.full_stack_test_available: A developer can reproduce the current Redis full-stack runtime plus current hardening corechain by checking out the refs pinned in this manifest.
acceptance.frozen_branch_preserved: redis-dash-0-0-2 remains a stable known branch and is not used as a future development base.
